---
title: "Joint convexity of the Umegaki quantum relative entropy"
subtitle: "A conventional-mathematics proof, in exact correspondence with the Lean formalization `qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity` (PhysLean)"
author: "Prepared for review — PhysLean contribution"
date: last-modified
abstract: |
  We give a self-contained, conventional-mathematics proof that the Umegaki quantum
  relative entropy is jointly convex. The argument is presented exactly as it is
  formalized in Lean 4 in the PhysLean library (theorem `qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity`,
  commit `bb18f8dc`): every step below corresponds to an identifiable step in the formal
  proof, and §7 gives the full paper-to-Lean dictionary. The proof route is the
  Frank–Lieb / Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta variational argument for the sandwiched Rényi
  trace functional, taken to the limit $\alpha \to 1^{+}$; it deliberately avoids
  differentiability at $\alpha=1$, using instead a one-sided convergent majorant
  (Lemma E, the one genuinely new ingredient) that requires only continuity plus two
  elementary scalar bounds. The purpose of this paper is to let a reviewer confirm the
  *mathematical* correctness of the contribution in ordinary language, independently of
  reading Lean, while §7 makes the correspondence to the machine-checked proof precise.
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    # Headings already carry manual numbers ("## 1.", "## 2." ...). Keep this false,
    # or xelatex prints the auto-number on top of the manual one ("1 1.", "2 2.").
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=1in
    colorlinks: true
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}
        \usepackage{unicode-math}
        \setmathfont{STIX Two Math}
        \usepackage{newunicodechar}
        \newunicodechar{𝐃}{\ensuremath{\mathbf{D}}}
        \newunicodechar{ℝ}{\ensuremath{\mathbb{R}}}
        \newunicodechar{ℂ}{\ensuremath{\mathbb{C}}}
fig-cap-location: bottom
---


This note renders the Lean proof of `qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity`
(`QuantumInfo/Entropy/DPI.lean`, line ~1488 as of commit `bb18f8dc`) as a mathematician
would write it on paper. The structure of the argument below follows the Lean proof
step for step; Lean identifiers are given in `code font` at the point where they are
used. Nothing is included that is not in the formal proof, and the two pre-existing
ingredients it relies on (joint convexity of the trace functional, and continuity in
$\alpha$) are stated as propositions with their own provenance.

## 1. Setting and conventions

Throughout, $d$ is a finite index set and states live on $\mathbb{C}^d$.

- A **state** (`MState d`, `QuantumInfo/States/Mixed/MState.lean:63`) is a Hermitian
  matrix $\rho \ge 0$ with $\operatorname{Tr}\rho = 1$.
- For $A \ge 0$ and $r \in \mathbb{R}$, the power $A^r$ is defined by functional
  calculus with $0^r := 0$ for $r \neq 0$ (`HermitianMat.rpow`,
  `QuantumInfo/ForMathlib/HermitianMat/Rpow.lean:45`); negative powers are therefore
  Moore–Penrose-style powers on the support. Likewise $\log A$ is taken on the support.
- We write $\operatorname{supp}\rho \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma$ for the support
  (= range) inclusion of PSD matrices; in Lean this is the kernel inclusion
  `σ.M.ker ≤ ρ.M.ker`.
- Entropies take values in $[0, \infty]$ (`ℝ≥0∞`), with the conventions
  $a \cdot \infty = \infty$ for $a \neq 0$ and $0 \cdot \infty = 0$.
- $p$ ranges over `Prob` $= \{p \in \mathbb{R} \mid 0 \le p \le 1\}$
  (`QuantumInfo/ClassicalInfo/Prob.lean:36`). The mixture notation `p [ρ₁ ↔ ρ₂]`
  (`Mixable.mix`, `Prob.lean:267`) denotes the state whose matrix is
  $p\rho_1 + (1-p)\rho_2$. In the theorem statement, `1 - p` on the right-hand side is
  truncated subtraction in `ℝ≥0∞`, which equals the real $1-p$ since $p \le 1$.
- $\log$ is the natural logarithm.

## 2. Definitions

**Definition 1 (Umegaki relative entropy; `qRelativeEnt`, notation `𝐃`,
`Relative.lean:1497`).**
$$
D(\rho\,\|\,\sigma) \;=\;
\begin{cases}
\operatorname{Tr}\!\bigl[\rho\,(\log\rho - \log\sigma)\bigr]
  & \text{if } \operatorname{supp}\rho \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma,\\[2pt]
+\infty & \text{otherwise.}
\end{cases}
$$
(In Lean, `𝐃(ρ‖σ)` is *defined* as `D̃_1(ρ‖σ)`, the $\alpha = 1$ member of the next
definition; the identification with the trace formula under the support condition is
`qRelativeEnt_ker`, `Relative.lean:1782`.)

**Definition 2 (sandwiched Rényi relative entropy; `SandwichedRelRentropy`, notation
`D̃_α`, `Relative.lean:1480`).** For $\alpha > 0$, set
$\gamma := \frac{1-\alpha}{2\alpha}$. If
$\operatorname{supp}\rho \not\subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma$, then
$\widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) := +\infty$. Otherwise
$$
\widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\,\|\,\sigma) \;:=\;
\begin{cases}
\dfrac{1}{\alpha - 1}\,
  \log \operatorname{Tr}\!\bigl[(\sigma^{\gamma}\rho\,\sigma^{\gamma})^{\alpha}\bigr]
  & \alpha \neq 1,\\[6pt]
\operatorname{Tr}\!\bigl[\rho\,(\log\rho - \log\sigma)\bigr] & \alpha = 1,
\end{cases}
$$
a nonnegative quantity (`sandwichedRelRentropy_nonneg`). (For $\alpha \le 0$ the Lean
definition returns the junk value $0$; only $\alpha \ge 1$ is used here.)

**Definition 3 (sandwiched trace functional; `sandwichedTraceFunctional`, notation
`Q̃_α`, `DPI.lean:66`).** For $\alpha \in \mathbb{R}$ and
$\gamma = \frac{1-\alpha}{2\alpha}$,
$$
\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\,\|\,\sigma) \;:=\;
\operatorname{Tr}\!\bigl[(\sigma^{\gamma}\rho\,\sigma^{\gamma})^{\alpha}\bigr]
\;\in\; \mathbb{R}.
$$
For $0 < \alpha \neq 1$ and $\operatorname{supp}\rho \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma$
one has (`sandwichedRelRentropy_eq_log_traceFunctional`, `DPI.lean:78`)
$$
\widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma)
= \Bigl[\tfrac{1}{\alpha-1}\log \widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma)\Bigr]^{+}
\quad\text{(as an element of } [0,\infty],\ \text{via ofReal)}.
$$

## 3. Theorem

**Theorem (`qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity`, `DPI.lean:1488`).**
For all states $\rho_1, \rho_2, \sigma_1, \sigma_2$ on $\mathbb{C}^d$ and all
$p \in [0,1]$, writing $\bar\rho := p\rho_1 + (1-p)\rho_2$ and
$\bar\sigma := p\sigma_1 + (1-p)\sigma_2$,
$$
D(\bar\rho\,\|\,\bar\sigma) \;\le\;
p\,D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) \;+\; (1-p)\,D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2)
$$
as an inequality in $[0,\infty]$.

```lean
theorem qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity :
    ∀ (ρ₁ ρ₂ σ₁ σ₂ : MState d), ∀ (p : Prob),
      𝐃(p [ρ₁ ↔ ρ₂]‖p [σ₁ ↔ σ₂]) ≤ p * 𝐃(ρ₁‖σ₁) + (1 - p) * 𝐃(ρ₂‖σ₂)
```

## 4. Ingredients

The proof consumes two substantial pre-existing results (A, B), one elementary matrix
lemma (C), and two scalar inequalities from Mathlib (D). Only Lemma E and the assembly
in §5 are new in this contribution.

**Proposition A (joint convexity of $\widetilde Q_\alpha$, $\alpha > 1$;
`sandwichedTraceFunctional_jointly_convex`, `DPI.lean:568`, pre-existing).**
Let $\alpha > 1$, let $\iota$ be a finite index set, let $w_i \ge 0$ with
$\sum_i w_i = 1$, and let $(\rho_i)$, $(\sigma_i)$ be states with
$\operatorname{supp}\rho_i \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma_i$ for every $i$. If
$\bar\rho, \bar\sigma$ are states with $\bar\rho = \sum_i w_i \rho_i$ and
$\bar\sigma = \sum_i w_i \sigma_i$ as matrices, then
$$
\widetilde Q_\alpha(\bar\rho\,\|\,\bar\sigma) \;\le\;
\sum_i w_i\, \widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho_i\|\sigma_i).
$$
*Proof route (already formalized; sketch only).* For $\alpha > 1$ and
$\operatorname{supp}\rho \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma$ one has the variational
formula (`traceFunctional_eq_iSup_f_alpha`, `DPI.lean:434`)
$$
\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) = \sup_{H \ge 0} f_\alpha(H, \rho, \sigma),
\qquad
f_\alpha(H,\rho,\sigma) = \alpha \operatorname{Tr}[\rho H]
  - (\alpha - 1) \operatorname{Tr}\!\bigl[(\sigma^{-\gamma} H
  \sigma^{-\gamma})^{\alpha/(\alpha-1)}\bigr]
$$
(`f_alpha`, `DPI.lean:174`), the supremum being attained at
$\hat H = \sigma^{\gamma}(\sigma^{\gamma}\rho\,\sigma^{\gamma})^{\alpha-1}\sigma^{\gamma}$.
For fixed $H \ge 0$, $f_\alpha$ is linear in $\rho$ and convex in $\sigma$: with
$s := -\gamma = \frac{\alpha-1}{2\alpha} \in (0, \tfrac12)$ and
$q := \frac{\alpha}{\alpha-1} > 1$, the map
$\sigma \mapsto \operatorname{Tr}[(\sigma^{s} H \sigma^{s})^{q}]$ is concave on the PSD
cone (`HermitianMat.trace_conj_rpow_concave`,
`QuantumInfo/ForMathlib/HermitianMat/LiebConcavity.lean:494`, itself derived from the
Lieb–Ando trace theorems formalized in
`QuantumInfo/ForMathlib/HayataGroup/TraceInequality/LiebAndoTrace.lean`), and it enters
$f_\alpha$ with the negative coefficient $-(\alpha-1)$. Hence each
$f_\alpha(H,\cdot,\cdot)$ is jointly convex (`f_alpha_jointly_convex`, `DPI.lean:494`),
and a pointwise supremum of jointly convex functions is jointly convex
(`iSup_f_alpha_jointly_convex`, `DPI.lean:527`). This is the Frank–Lieb variational
argument in the form used by Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta; see the literature note. $\square$

**Proposition B (continuity in $\alpha$; `sandwichedRelRentropy.continuousOn`,
`Relative.lean:1769`, pre-existing).** For fixed states $\rho, \sigma$, the map
$\alpha \mapsto \widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma)$ is continuous on $(0,\infty)$
(with respect to the order topology on $[0,\infty]$). The consequence used below is:
$$
\widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) \longrightarrow \widetilde D_1(\rho\|\sigma)
= D(\rho\|\sigma) \qquad (\alpha \to 1^{+}).
$$
Note that since $\widetilde D_1 := D$ *by definition*, the $\alpha \to 1$ convergence of
the sandwiched Rényi entropy to the Umegaki entropy is precisely the content of this
continuity theorem; it is a genuine analytic fact proved earlier in the library, not a
definitional triviality.

**Lemma C (kernels of weighted sums; `HermitianMat.ker_weighted_sum_le`,
`DPI.lean:545`).** If $w_i \ge 0$, all $\rho_i, \sigma_i \ge 0$, and
$\operatorname{supp}\rho_i \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma_i$ for all $i$, then
$\operatorname{supp}\bigl(\sum_i w_i\rho_i\bigr) \subseteq
\operatorname{supp}\bigl(\sum_i w_i\sigma_i\bigr)$.

**Scalar facts D (Mathlib).**
(i) $\log x \le x - 1$ for $x > 0$ (`Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos`).
(ii) $|e^x - 1 - x| \le x^2$ for $|x| \le 1$ (`Real.abs_exp_sub_one_sub_id_le`); in
particular $e^x - 1 \le x + x^2$ there.

Also used: strict positivity $\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) > 0$ under the support
condition (`sandwichedTraceFunctional_pos`, `DPI.lean:100`).

## 5. The key new lemma: a convergent majorant for the difference quotient

**Lemma E (`sandwichedTraceFunctional_sub_one_div_eventually_le`, `DPI.lean:1425`,
new).** Let $\rho, \sigma$ be states with
$\operatorname{supp}\rho \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma$ (so
$D(\rho\|\sigma) < \infty$). Then there is a function $u : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$
with
$$
u(\alpha) \xrightarrow[\alpha \to 1^{+}]{} D(\rho\|\sigma)
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
\frac{\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) - 1}{\alpha - 1} \;\le\; u(\alpha)
\quad\text{for all } \alpha > 1 \text{ sufficiently close to } 1 .
$$

*Proof.* For $\alpha > 1$ put
$$
r(\alpha) := \frac{\log \widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma)}{\alpha - 1},
\qquad
x(\alpha) := (\alpha - 1)\, r(\alpha) = \log \widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma),
$$
and define $u(\alpha) := r(\alpha) + x(\alpha)\, r(\alpha)
= r(\alpha) + (\alpha-1)r(\alpha)^2$.

1. Under the support condition, $r(\alpha) \ge 0$ and
   $\widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) = [r(\alpha)]^{+}$ (Definition 3 with
   `sandwichedRelRentropy_nonneg`), and $D(\rho\|\sigma) \neq \infty$. By Proposition B,
   $r(\alpha) \to D(\rho\|\sigma)$ (as real numbers) as $\alpha \to 1^{+}$
   (Lean: `h_r_tendsto`, passing through `ENNReal.tendsto_toReal`).
2. Hence $x(\alpha) = (\alpha-1) r(\alpha) \to 0$, so eventually $|x(\alpha)| \le 1$
   (Lean: `h_eps`, `h_small`).
3. $\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) > 0$ (positivity above), so
   $\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) = e^{x(\alpha)}$. By D(ii), for the eventual
   $\alpha$ of step 2,
   $\widetilde Q_\alpha - 1 \le x(\alpha) + x(\alpha)^2$; dividing by $\alpha - 1 > 0$,
   $$
   \frac{\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) - 1}{\alpha - 1}
   \;\le\; r(\alpha) + x(\alpha)\, r(\alpha) \;=\; u(\alpha).
   $$
4. By steps 1–2, $u(\alpha) \to D(\rho\|\sigma) + 0 \cdot D(\rho\|\sigma) =
   D(\rho\|\sigma)$. $\blacksquare$

*Heuristic remark (not part of the formal proof).* Since
$\widetilde Q_1(\rho\|\sigma) = \operatorname{Tr}\rho = 1$, the quantity
$(\widetilde Q_\alpha - 1)/(\alpha - 1)$ is a difference quotient whose two-sided limit
"should" be $\frac{d}{d\alpha}\widetilde Q_\alpha\big|_{\alpha=1} = D(\rho\|\sigma)$. The
formal proof deliberately avoids establishing differentiability: a one-sided *eventual
upper bound by a convergent majorant* suffices for the limit argument, and that follows
from continuity of $\widetilde D_\alpha$ alone, via the two elementary scalar bounds.

## 6. Proof of the theorem

Fix $\rho_1,\rho_2,\sigma_1,\sigma_2$ and $p$. The proof splits exactly as the Lean
source does.

**Step 0 — degenerate weights.** If $p = 0$, then $\bar\rho = \rho_2$,
$\bar\sigma = \sigma_2$ (in Lean via `Mixable.mix_zero`), and the right-hand side is
$0 \cdot D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) + 1 \cdot D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2) = D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2)$ — note
this uses $0 \cdot \infty = 0$, so the case is genuine, not vacuous. Equality holds.
If $p = 1$, symmetrically $\bar\rho = \rho_1$, $\bar\sigma = \sigma_1$ (the mixture
identity `1 [x₁ ↔ x₂] = x₁` is proved inline through injectivity of the underlying-matrix
map, `Mixable.to_U_inj`), and the right side is $D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1)$.
Henceforth $0 < p < 1$ (Lean: `hp0'`, `hp1'`).

**Step 1 — infinite right-hand side.** Suppose
$\operatorname{supp}\rho_1 \not\subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma_1$ (Lean: `hker₁`
fails). Then $D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) = \infty$ by Definition 1, and since $p \neq 0$,
$p \cdot \infty = \infty$, so the right-hand side is $\infty$ and the inequality is
trivial (`le_top`). The case
$\operatorname{supp}\rho_2 \not\subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma_2$ is identical,
using $1 - p \neq 0$. So we may assume both support conditions
$$
\operatorname{supp}\rho_i \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\sigma_i \quad (i = 1,2),
$$
whence $D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1),\, D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2) < \infty$ (Lean: `h_ne₁`, `h_ne₂`).

**Step 2 — setup for the main case.** Write the mixtures as weighted sums over
$\iota = \{1,2\}$ (Lean: `Fin 2`) with weight vector $w = (p,\, 1-p)$:
$$
\bar\rho = \textstyle\sum_i w_i \rho_i, \qquad \bar\sigma = \sum_i w_i \sigma_i
$$
as matrix identities (Lean: `hM_ρ`, `hM_σ`, by unfolding `Mixable.mix`); $w_i \ge 0$ and
$\sum_i w_i = 1$ (`hw_nonneg`, `hw_sum`). By Lemma C,
$\operatorname{supp}\bar\rho \subseteq \operatorname{supp}\bar\sigma$ (Lean:
`hker_mix`). Apply Lemma E to each pair to obtain majorants $u_1, u_2$ with
$u_i(\alpha) \to D(\rho_i\|\sigma_i)$ as $\alpha \to 1^{+}$ and, eventually,
$(\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho_i\|\sigma_i) - 1)/(\alpha-1) \le u_i(\alpha)$.

**Step 3 — pointwise bound for $\alpha > 1$ (Lean: `h_ev`).** For $\alpha > 1$ in the
eventual range of both majorants, using $\widetilde Q_\alpha(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma) > 0$
(positivity, with `hker_mix`) and Definition 3:
$$
\widetilde D_\alpha(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma)
= \Bigl[\frac{\log \widetilde Q_\alpha(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma)}{\alpha-1}\Bigr]^{+},
$$
and we bound the bracketed real number:
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{\log \widetilde Q_\alpha(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma)}{\alpha - 1}
&\;\le\;
\frac{\widetilde Q_\alpha(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma) - 1}{\alpha - 1}
&&\text{[D(i): } \log x \le x - 1\text{]}
\\[2pt]
&\;\le\;
\frac{p\,\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho_1\|\sigma_1)
      + (1{-}p)\,\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho_2\|\sigma_2) - 1}{\alpha - 1}
&&\text{[Proposition A, } \iota = \{1,2\}\text{]}
\\[2pt]
&\;=\;
p\,\frac{\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) - 1}{\alpha - 1}
+ (1{-}p)\,\frac{\widetilde Q_\alpha(\rho_2\|\sigma_2) - 1}{\alpha - 1}
&&\text{[since } p + (1{-}p) = 1\text{]}
\\[2pt]
&\;\le\; p\, u_1(\alpha) + (1{-}p)\, u_2(\alpha)
&&\text{[Lemma E].}
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, eventually as $\alpha \to 1^{+}$,
$$
\widetilde D_\alpha(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma) \;\le\;
\bigl[\,p\, u_1(\alpha) + (1{-}p)\, u_2(\alpha)\,\bigr]^{+}.
$$

**Step 4 — pass to the limit $\alpha \to 1^{+}$.** By Proposition B (applied to the
mixture pair), the left side tends to $D(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma)$ (Lean: `h_lhs`). By
Lemma E and continuity of $t \mapsto [t]^{+}$, the right side tends to
$$
\bigl[\,p\,D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) + (1{-}p)\,D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2)\,\bigr]^{+}
\;=\; p\,D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) + (1{-}p)\,D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2)
$$
(Lean: `h_rhs`; the identification of the $[\,\cdot\,]^{+}$ of the real convex
combination with the $[0,\infty]$-valued convex combination uses the finiteness from
Step 1, via `ENNReal.ofReal_toReal` and friends). Since the inequality of Step 3 holds
eventually along the (nontrivial) filter of right-neighbourhoods of $1$, the inequality
passes to the limits (`le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto`):
$$
D(\bar\rho\|\bar\sigma) \;\le\; p\,D(\rho_1\|\sigma_1) + (1{-}p)\,D(\rho_2\|\sigma_2).
\qquad\blacksquare
$$

## 7. Lean–paper dictionary

| Paper object / step | Lean identifier | Location (commit `bb18f8dc`) |
|---|---|---|
| Umegaki $D$ | `qRelativeEnt` / `𝐃` | `Relative.lean:1497` |
| Sandwiched $\widetilde D_\alpha$ | `SandwichedRelRentropy` / `D̃_α` | `Relative.lean:1480` |
| Trace functional $\widetilde Q_\alpha$ | `sandwichedTraceFunctional` / `Q̃_α` | `DPI.lean:66` |
| $\widetilde D_\alpha = [\log \widetilde Q_\alpha / (\alpha{-}1)]^{+}$ | `sandwichedRelRentropy_eq_log_traceFunctional` | `DPI.lean:78` |
| Proposition A | `sandwichedTraceFunctional_jointly_convex` | `DPI.lean:568` |
| — variational formula | `traceFunctional_eq_iSup_f_alpha`, `f_alpha`, `H_hat` | `DPI.lean:434, 174, 180` |
| — concavity in $\sigma$ | `HermitianMat.trace_conj_rpow_concave` | `LiebConcavity.lean:494` |
| Proposition B | `sandwichedRelRentropy.continuousOn` | `Relative.lean:1769` |
| Lemma C | `HermitianMat.ker_weighted_sum_le` | `DPI.lean:545` |
| D(i) | `Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos` | Mathlib |
| D(ii) | `Real.abs_exp_sub_one_sub_id_le` | Mathlib |
| $\widetilde Q_\alpha > 0$ | `sandwichedTraceFunctional_pos` | `DPI.lean:100` |
| Lemma E | `sandwichedTraceFunctional_sub_one_div_eventually_le` | `DPI.lean:1425` |
| Steps 0–4 | `qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity` | `DPI.lean:1488` |
| Step 3 | `h_ev` (with `h_convex`) | `DPI.lean:1572–1594` |
| Step 4 | `h_lhs`, `h_rhs`, `le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto` | `DPI.lean:1548–1595` |

## 8. Remarks for the reviewer

- **What is new.** Lemma E and the theorem itself (§5–§6), ~190 lines added to
  `DPI.lean`; the previously `@[sorryful]` statement was removed from `Relative.lean`.
  Propositions A and B and all deeper inputs (Lieb–Ando trace theorems) were already in
  the library. The theorem was placed in `DPI.lean` rather than `Relative.lean` because
  the $\widetilde Q_\alpha$ machinery lives there and importing it from `Relative.lean`
  would create a cycle.
- **No differentiability is needed.** The classical way to see
  $\widetilde D_\alpha \to D$ and to transfer convexity is to differentiate
  $\alpha \mapsto \widetilde Q_\alpha$ at $\alpha = 1$; the formal proof replaces this by
  the one-sided majorant of Lemma E, which needs only continuity (Proposition B) plus the
  scalar bounds D. This is the main proof-engineering choice.
- **The statement is the binary form.** Finite mixtures of $n$ states follow by
  induction in the usual way, but only the $n$-ary form of Proposition A (arbitrary
  finite `Fintype ι`) is formalized; the $\mathbf{D}$-level statement is for two pairs,
  matching the pre-existing `sorryful` statement it closes.
- **Axioms.** The proof is `sorry`-free; `#print axioms qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity`
  reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` (recorded in the commit
  message of `bb18f8dc`).
